---
title: "Knitr Basics"
author: "Jane Doe"
date: "March 22nd, 2021"
toc: true
format:
  html:
    html-math-method: katex
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

::: {.callout-tip}
## Learn more
See the full guide on [Using R with Quarto](https://quarto.org/docs/computations/r.html) and [Knitr Code Cells](https://quarto.org/docs/reference/cells/cells-knitr.html).
:::

## Introduction

This is an R Markdown document. Markdown is a simple formatting syntax for authoring HTML, PDF, and MS Word documents. For more details on using R Markdown see <http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com>.

When you click the **Knit** button a document will be generated that includes both content as well as the output of any embedded R code chunks within the document. You can embed an R code chunk like this:

```{r cars}
summary(cars)
```

## Plot Output

You can also embed plots, for example:

```{r pressure, message=FALSE}
#| code-fold: true
library(ggplot2)
dat <- data.frame(cond = rep(c("A", "B"), each=10),
                  xvar = 1:20 + rnorm(20,sd=3),
                  yvar = 1:20 + rnorm(20,sd=3))

ggplot(dat, aes(x=xvar, y=yvar)) +
    geom_point(shape=1) + 
    geom_smooth() 
```

Note that the `code-fold: true` parameter was added to the code chunk to hide the code by default (click the "Code" above to see the code).

## Interactive Plots

You can also add interactive plots. For example:

```{r, fig.width=7, fig.height=4}
library(dygraphs)
dygraph(nhtemp, main = "New Haven Temperatures") %>% 
  dyRangeSelector(dateWindow = c("1920-01-01", "1960-01-01"))
```

## LaTeX Math

You can also include LaTeX math:

$$
P\left(A=2\middle|\frac{A^2}{B}>4\right)
$$
